name: Le Gateau Victoire Au Chocolat, Mousseline
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Heavy or whipping cream, chilled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and place rack in the lower-third level.
2. Prepare a square pan (9x9x2 inches) or any 10-cup cake pan with nonstick lining, buttered, bottom lined with buttered waxed paper, and floured.
3. In a medium saucepan, stir together coffee and hot water. Add rum and break up chocolate in the pan. Bring to a boil in a larger pan, remove from heat, and set the chocolate pan in it; cover and let chocolate melt while you continue with the recipe.
4. Beat eggs and sugar in a beating bowl over hot water for several minutes until eggs are slightly warm to your finger (this makes beating faster and increases volume). Then beat five minutes or more until mixture has at least tripled in volume and forms a thick ribbon when lifted and falls from beater. The eggs should be the consistency of lightly whipped cream.
5. Pour cream into a metal mixing bowl. Place ice cubes in a larger bowl, cover with cold water, then set the cream bowl into the larger ice-filled bowl. Beat until cream has doubled in volume and holds its shape softly. Whip in vanilla.
6. Beat up melted chocolate until smooth and silky. Scrape into egg-sugar mixture, blending rapidly with a rubber spatula, and when partially incorporated, fold in whipped cream, deflating as little as possible. Pour batter into prepared cake pan (about two-thirds filled).
7. Place pan in hot water in the preheated oven. Cake will rise slightly above the edge of the pan and be done after about one hour of baking. Turn off the oven, leave the door ajar, and let cake sit for 30 minutes in its pan of water so it can sink evenly.
8. Remove from oven still in its pan of water and let sit another 30 minutes so it can firm up before unmolding and serving. Cake will sink down as it cools to about its original volume.
9. Unmold the cake and decorate with a sprinkling of confectioner's sugar or with pipings of whipped cream, or with a soft chocolate icing (semisweet chocolate melted and beaten with a little softened butter).
10. You may wish to pass a custard sauce or sweetened and vanilla-flavored whipped cream over the cake.
From "Julia Child & Company" 1978
